macro_segments.title: The Mishna on Remnants of Mincha: When the Kometz Can Be Brought Without Shirayim

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishna: Status of Kometz When Shirayim Are Lost, Burned, or Impure
The Debate Between Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Yehoshua: Must Something Remain for the Korban to Be Valid?
Rav's Limitation: Tum'a Problem Only When ALL Shirayim Become Impure
Gemara's Analysis: Does Tumah Have Special Status Due to Tzitz?

macro_segments.title: The Role of Tzitz and Priestly Service in Validating Sacrifices with Missing Parts

micro_segments.title: Does Tzitz Work 'Al Ha'Ochlin: Does It Permit Eating Impure Edible Parts?

macro_segments.title: The Mishna on Remnants of Mincha: When the Kometz Can Be Brought Without Shirayim (continued)

micro_segments.title: Clarification: Rabbi Yehoshua Needs Only a Portion Left, Not All Shirayim

macro_segments.title: The Role of Tzitz and Priestly Service in Validating Sacrifices with Missing Parts (continued)

micro_segments.title: Debate Over Tzitz Requirement: Must the Kohen Gadol Wear It or Is Presence Enough?

macro_segments.title: The Mishna on Remnants of Mincha: When the Kometz Can Be Brought Without Shirayim (continued) (continued)

micro_segments.title: Baraita on Rabbi Yehoshua: Kezayit of Meat or Fat Suffices for Zerikat HaDam

macro_segments.title: The Status of the Minchat Nesachim When the Animal's Meat Is Lost

micro_segments.title: Question: Can One Sprinkle Blood If Only the Accompanying Mincha Remains?
Rav Papa's Answer: Minchat Nesachim Does Not Count as Part of the Sacrifice Itself

macro_segments.title: The Requirement for Some Remnant to Remain and the Amount Necessary (Kezayit Standard)

micro_segments.title: Source for the Kezayit Requirement: Leriach Nichoach Implies Only Animal Parts Count
The Yoteret and Two Kidneys: Remnants Beyond Fat Also Permit Zerikat HaDam
Why Both 'Chelev' and 'Leriach Nichoach' Are Needed in the Pasuk

macro_segments.title: The Debate on Whether Kli Sharet Is Required After Taking the Kometz

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's Position: Rabbi Shimon's Logic from Comparison to Chatat and Asham
Rabbi Yannai's Extreme Position: No Kli Sharet Needed After Taking Kometz
Rav Nachman's Middle Position: Kli Sharet Required Initially But Not for Final Burning
Tosefta Support: Fat, Limbs, Wood, Kometz, Levona All Valid However Placed on Altar

macro_segments.title: The Debate on Using Left vs. Right Hand and Vessels When Burning the Kometz

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's Clarification: Right Hand without Kli or Left Hand with Kli, Not Interchangeable
Baraita: Taking From Kli Sharet, Putting in Kli Sharet, Burning From Kli Sharet
Rabbi Shimon vs. Chachamim: Can One Take From Kli Sharet Without Returning to Kli?
Supporting Baraita: Kometz Taken From Kli, Put in Kli, Burned From Kli; Shimon Disagrees
Contradiction Proof: Switching Kometz to Left Hand Invalidates It (Chachamim View)
Explanation: Left Hand Without Kli Is Neither Valid Nor Fully Invalid; Must Return to Right

macro_segments.title: Burning the Kometz in Multiple Stages and the Kezayit Requirement

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Burning Kometz in Two Halves Is Valid; Debate Over Whether Quarters Are Permitted
Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi: Only Halves, Not Quarters; Each Part Must Be Kezayit
Rabbi Yochanan: Even Quarters Permitted; Haktara Valid Even If Less Than Kezayit Per Stage
The Core Debate: Can Haktara Be Valid If Each Individual Act Is Less Than Kezayit?

macro_segments.title: When Does the Kometz Become Valid: At First Ignition or When Fire Seizes Most of It?

micro_segments.title: Question: From What Point Does the Kometz Permit the Shirayim to Be Eaten?
Rabbi Chanina: Valid When Fire First Takes Hold (Timshila)
Rabbi Yochanan: Valid Only When Majority Is Seized by Fire (Tatzi be-Rubo)
Source: Oven Metaphor—Smoke Rises Only When Fire Seizes Majority of Contents
Rav Amram's Baraita: Things Burned at Night (Fats) vs. Day (Kometz, Levona, Incense)
Resolution: Putting on Fire Must Occur Before Sunset, But Majority-Burning Can Occur After
Conceptual Point: The Act vs. Its Completion; Comparison to Shabbat Melacha Timing

topical_tags.term: Rabbi Eliezer vs. Rabbi Yehoshua (blood/kometz validity)
Yoteret u-Shtei Klayot (lobe of liver and two kidneys)
Rabbi Shimon (lenient on kli sharet requirement)
Rabbi Yannai (most lenient on kometz process)
Rav Nachman (middle position on kli sharet)
Matir (enabling factor for next stage of service)
